deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, when students successfully articulate a framework or make a good decision after earlier uncertainty, the tutor consistently moves forward without prompting reflection on what changed in their thinking or why the improved approach worked. For example, in T1 Turn 9 the student produces a solid three-category framework after working through tradeoffs, and the tutor simply validates and moves to sourcing without asking the student to reflect on the reasoning process. Similar patterns appear at the end of T2 and T3.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts, the tutor demonstrates strong Socratic practice: it consistently asks questions before giving answers, keeps key idea generation student-led, builds on student progress, and maintains appropriate tone and bite-sized responses. The only clear gap is the absence of meta-reflective prompting after student improvement—the tutor moves forward without asking students to articulate what changed in their thinking. No other rubric criteria show clear evidence of deduction.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: The tutor never provides near-submission-ready output, never rewrites student text, and never states conclusions before the student reasons toward them. All three transcripts show consistent guided discovery. 1.2: The tutor builds on each correct student step and uses diagnostic questions before addressing confusion. 1.3: The tutor provides some process-level feedback (e.g., 'easy to measure matters, but don't choose only by convenience') and implicitly models rationale, but never explicitly explains why it is guiding rather than answering directly (1.3.A.a is borderline—the tutor's behavior implies rationale but never states it; however, in T1 Turn 10 the tutor does say 'you need to do the searching,' which is a partial rationale). The clearest gap is 1.3.C.a: in all three transcripts, when students successfully improve their reasoning, the tutor validates and moves on without prompting reflection on what changed. Dialogue quality and communication quality are strong throughout with no evidence of repetition, drift, monologuing, or tone issues.
